content.problemStatement:
You are given an array AAA consisting of NNN integers and an integer XXX.

Find the **maximum** element in each subarray of size XXX.

Print (N−X+1)(N-X+1)(N−X+1) space-separated integers where the ithi^{th}ith integer denotes the maximum element of the ithi^{th}ith subarray of size XXX from left.


Input Format

The first line of input will contain a single integer TTT, denoting the number of test cases.
Each test case consists of multiple lines of input.

The first line of each test case contains two space-separated integers NNN and XXX — the number of elements in the array and the size of subarrays.
The next line contains NNN space-separated integers, the elements of array AAA.




Output Format
For each test case, output on a new line, the **maximum** element in each subarray of size XXX.

Print (N−X+1)(N-X+1)(N−X+1) space-separated integers where the ithi^{th}ith integer denotes the maximum element of the ithi^{th}ith subarray of size XXX from left.
constraints: Sum of N over all test cases ≤ 2·10^5
content.inputFormat:
The first line contains an integer T, the number of test cases. For each test case:
- The first line contains two integers N and X, the length of the array and the window size.
- The second line contains N space‑separated integers A1 … AN.
content.outputFormat: For each test case output a single line with N‑X+1 space‑separated integers. The i‑th integer is the maximum of the subarray A[i] … A[i+X‑1].

sampleTestCases.explanation: Test case 1: windows of size 2 → [1,2]=2, [2,3]=3, [3,4]=4.\nTest case 2: windows of size 3 → [4,3,2]=4, [3,2,1]=3.\nTest case 3: window size 1 returns the original array.
Window size equals array length, so the maximum of the whole array is 9.

solution.approach:
Maintain a double‑ended queue (deque) that stores indices of elements in non‑increasing order of their values. While scanning the array:
1. Remove indices that are out of the current window (i‑X).
2. Pop from the back while the current element is larger than the element at those indices – they can never become a maximum.
3. Push the current index.
4. When the first window is complete (i ≥ X‑1), the element at the front of the deque is the maximum for that window.
This yields O(N) time per test case and O(N) auxiliary space (the deque holds at most N indices, but never more than X at any moment).
solution.timeComplexity: O(N) per test case, where N is the length of the array.
solution.spaceComplexity: O(N) in the worst case for the deque (actually O(X) because indices older than the window are removed).
